name: Take Charge America
description_short: NFCC-affiliated nonprofit offering free credit counseling, debt management plans, and financial education to help consumers eliminate unsecured debt without loans or settlement.
description_long:
Take Charge America is a nonprofit financial counseling organization providing free expert guidance to consumers struggling with debt. Founded to address the growing need for accessible financial education, the organization operates as a debt management resource rather than a lending or settlement company, explicitly stating "Take Charge America, Inc. is NOT A LOAN COMPANY. We do not lend money." They operate during extended business hours (Mon-Fri 6 a.m. - 6 p.m. MST) with separate phone lines for first-time clients (866-750-9618) and existing clients (866-528-0588).

Take Charge America offers comprehensive debt help services including one-on-one credit counseling, debt management plans, housing assistance, bankruptcy education certificates, and budgeting tools. Clients can access free online counseling sessions and receive personalized debt estimates showing potential savings through their programs. The organization provides an interactive debt calculator demonstrating estimated monthly payment reductions, interest rate decreases (example: from 25% to 8%), and typical 3-5 year repayment timelines. They also maintain an education center with webinars, money lessons, calculators, and financial resources.

The company distinguishes itself through genuine nonprofit status, professional counseling staff focused on consumer benefit rather than profit, and recognition of life circumstances with supportive, non-judgmental guidance. Their service model emphasizes customizable payment dates, potential fee waivers from creditors, and elimination of collection calls through formal debt management enrollment. Customer testimonials highlight impeccable customer service, knowledgeable staff, and transparent communication about realistic debt payoff options.

A key caveat is that actual results depend heavily on creditor participation in debt management plans—not all creditors accept negotiated terms. The minimum debt requirement ($2,000 unsecured debt) excludes those with minimal debt loads. While free initial counseling is available, clients should understand this is credit counseling and debt management planning, not debt settlement or loan consolidation, meaning full debt repayment remains the goal rather than reducing principal owed.
pros: Completely free credit counseling with no upfront costs or fees
NFCC-affiliated nonprofit status with transparent, consumer-focused mission
Extended availability (6 a.m. - 6 p.m. MST) with dedicated support lines for new vs. existing clients
Interactive debt estimation calculator showing specific savings projections and 3-5 year payoff timelines
Debt management plans with potential creditor fee waivers and interest rate reductions
Comprehensive financial education including webinars, budgeting tools, and money lesson plans
Emphasis on customizable solutions and supportive counseling approach rather than judgment
cons: Success depends on creditor participation in debt management plans—not guaranteed for all accounts
Minimum $2,000 unsecured debt requirement excludes consumers with smaller debt loads
Debt management plans require full repayment over 3-5 years (no principal reduction like debt settlement)
Limited to unsecured debt; does not address mortgages, auto loans, or other secured obligations
Requires commitment to structured payment plans with specific payment dates and amounts
best_for: Consumers with $5,000-$100,000+ in credit card or unsecured debt seeking structured repayment without loans
Individuals wanting free, professional counseling before considering bankruptcy or debt settlement
First-time homebuyers or renters needing housing education and credit improvement guidance
People struggling with collection accounts who want creditor negotiation support
diagnosis: Take Charge America is best for consumers with $5,000+ in unsecured debt who want legitimate nonprofit counseling, structured repayment plans, and creditor negotiation without taking on new loans or settlement debt. The primary caveat is that this is credit counseling and debt management, not debt reduction—clients repay full balances over 3-5 years with improved terms, not reduced principal, and success depends on creditors accepting the payment plan.
services: Debt management plans with creditor negotiation
Housing assistance and foreclosure/eviction prevention counseling
Debt estimation and savings projection calculator
Financial education webinars and money lessons
Consumer resource library and financial tips blog
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 20430 N 19Th Ave, Suite B155, Phoenix, AZ 85027
